title: Superheating field of clean superconductors near the type-I--type-II boundary: the low-temperature Meissner stability limit of niobium
abstract: We calculate the low-temperature superheating field $B_{\rm sh}$ of clean superconductors near the boundary between type-I and type-II superconductivity, with particular emphasis on Nb. The calculation is based on the self-consistent nonlinear nonlocal Eilenberger theory and the linear stability analysis of the Meissner state. For a Nb-like material with $κ_{\rm GL}=0.7$, we obtain $B_{\rm sh}\simeq 290\,{\rm mT}$ at $T/T_c=0.2$, using $B_{c0}\simeq 200\,{\rm mT}$. This value is substantially higher than the value obtained by naively extrapolating the Ginzburg--Landau result near $T_c$ to $T\ll T_c$. For a TESLA-shaped Nb accelerator cavity, it corresponds to an intrinsic Meissner-stability limit of about $67\,{\rm MV/m}$.
summary: This theoretical paper calculates the intrinsic superheating field limit ($B_{sh}$) for clean superconductors like Niobium using advanced Eilenberger theory. The result provides a significantly higher estimate for the Meissner stability limit compared to simpler Ginzburg-Landau extrapolations. This finding is crucial for designing high-performance superconducting accelerator cavities.
main_problem: The paper calculates the low-temperature superheating field ($B_{sh}$) of clean superconductors near the type-I–type-II boundary.
main_result: For Niobium (Nb) with $\kappa_{GL}=0.7$, the calculated $B_{sh}$ is $\simeq 290 	ext{ mT}$ at $T/T_c=0.2$, corresponding to an intrinsic Meissner-stability limit of about $67 	ext{ MV/m}$.
method: The calculation employs a self-consistent nonlinear nonlocal Eilenberger theory combined with linear stability analysis of the Meissner state.
model_or_system: The study focuses on clean superconductors, specifically Niobium (Nb), relevant for Superconducting Radio-Frequency (SRF) accelerator cavities operating in the Meissner state. The theoretical framework handles non-local current response.
main_assumptions: The stability analysis is assumed to be set by the one-dimensional instability ($ ar{k}=0$). The calculation is limited to $T/T_c \ge 0.1$.
figures_summary: Figure 1(a) compares Eilenberger results to GL values for $B_{sh}(T)/B_c(T)$ vs. temperature, while Figure 1(b) shows $B_{sh}$ dependence on $\kappa_{GL}$ near $T_c$.
paper_structure: The paper develops the superheating-field problem using Eilenberger theory, compares results to GL approximations, calculates $B_{sh}(T)$ across the temperature range, and applies this limit to practical accelerator applications.
why_interesting: While focused on superconductivity, the rigorous nonlocal stability analysis techniques developed here (Eilenberger formalism) are highly sophisticated many-body tools that could inform models of quantum interfaces or non-equilibrium dynamics in other condensed matter systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Temperature dependence of the superheating ﬁeld for a clean low-κ superconductor. Filled symbols show Eilenberger results at ﬁxed κ0; the corresponding κGL values are obtained from the near-Tc mapping. Open symbols at T/Tc = 1 show the corresponding GL values, which are valid in the limit T →Tc. (b) Superheating ﬁeld near Tc as a function of κGL. The agreement with the GL result provides a check of the Eilenberger calculation in the near-Tc limit.
